workflow_name: Customer Escalation Email -> Risk Brief + Manager Loop
user_message: Just got this escalation from Wide World Importers. Their Azure SQL is down and they're saying it's been 6 hours. I need to loop in my manager AND get the support team on this -- can you help me triage and draft the communications?

input_artifacts.content:
From: Patricia Doyle <patricia.doyle@wideworldimporters.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 14:23 EST
Subject: URGENT - Production Database Down 6+ Hours

Raj,

I'm escalating this directly to you because support hasn't responded in 6 hours. Our production Azure SQL database has been unreachable since 8:15 AM. This is our order management system -- we're losing approximately $12,000/hour in orders.

Our DBA (Mike Chen) has tried everything on our end. Support ticket #SR-2024-88431 was opened at 8:30 AM and we've had zero updates.

I need someone at Microsoft to treat this as critical immediately. If this isn't resolved by 5 PM today, my CEO will be calling your VP.

Patricia Doyle
CTO, Wide World Importers

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Patricia Doyle <patricia.doyle@wideworldimporters.com>
Date: 2024-03-11 14:35 EST
Subject: Re: URGENT - Production Database Down 6+ Hours

Patricia,

I just saw this and I'm treating it as top priority. Let me immediately:
1. Escalate SR-2024-88431 internally
2. Get you a status update within 30 minutes
3. Loop in my manager for executive visibility

Stand by.

Raj

opportunity.name: WWI Azure SQL Premium Tier Upgrade

usability_anchors: Score 5: Produces severity assessment, manager brief, and customer draft -- but also flags that the support/delivery team engagement is OUTSIDE the skill's native routing (FM-WF34-N); explicitly notes the gap: 'escalation routing doesn't natively engage support/delivery teams' and proposes a workaround (manual escalation path, SR ticket escalation steps)
Score 3: Produces all artifacts correctly but doesn't acknowledge the routing gap; treats support engagement as if it's handled elsewhere without noting it
Score 1: Ignores the support team dimension entirely, producing only the manager brief and customer draft as if the technical resolution is someone else's problem
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Severity assessment uses pattern data (3rd escalation in 9 months, CTO escalating, CEO threat, renewal in 6 months, $12K/hr revenue impact); correctly notes SR-2024-88431 has no assignee after 6 hours
Score 3: Severity assessment correct but misses the escalation pattern (prior incidents) or the renewal timeline risk
Score 1: Severity assessment based only on the email tone without data correlation
decision_anchors: Score 5: For the design-redesign dimension -- produces a verdict on whether the current skill routing adequately serves this scenario; notes that routing to customer-meeting-prep-outreach-coach for escalation triage forces synthesis across modules when a native 'escalation playbook' would be more direct; next-step sequence includes stop conditions
Score 3: Correct operational output but no structural verdict on routing adequacy
Score 1: No acknowledgment of the routing gap; no stop conditions in sequence

guardrail_checks: Customer draft must NOT over-apologize or commit liability
Must NOT recommend commitments outside CSA/DSSP authority
Manager brief must NOT reuse customer-facing language
Must NOT expose internal blame in customer draft
